name: Second Harvest Food Bank
mission: Summer Meals Second Harvest works with school districts and community partners to maximize the use of federally funded school meals.
theoryOfChange: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley partnered with Project Evident to design a measurement strategy for their 'One Community Initiative'. This strategy included a theory of change for the initiative, aimed at understanding its impact on client experiences and, consequently, on food insecurity and hunger outcomes. The initiative serves as an incubator to test innovative approaches to improve programs and rigorously assess their impact for potential scaling across Second Harvest's service area.

scoreSummary: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley shows exceptional impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 74% of reported revenue is noncash (gifts-in-kind)
keyConcerns.detail: Noncash contributions inflate reported program ratios. Cash-adjusted metrics better reflect actual spending.

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.

score_details.score_summary: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley shows exceptional impact and good alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: No mention of "zakat" on their website or donation pages, nor any dedicated zakat funds or campaigns were found in the search results. The donation pages list various methods of giving (e.g., one-time, monthly, stock, crypto, DAF) but do not include zakat.

all_citations.claim: The organization holds a 100.0/100 rating and holds 9.5 months of working capital.
Total revenue reached $286,205,684 with a fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised.
The organization focuses on maximizing federally funded meals and food rescue operations.
The organization maintains a program expense ratio of 94.8%.
The charity tracks impact through average monthly clients served.
The organization accepts general charitable donations and sadaqah.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a GMG score of 69/100 due to its perfect financial health and transparency ratings, though its impact score is moderated by the high operational costs inherent to its geographic location.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary due to the extreme cost of living in the Silicon Valley service area
Opportunity to further diversify donor alignment for specific cultural or religious giving groups

dimension_explanations.alignment: Second Harvest addresses a cause of extreme urgency, focusing on food insecurity in a region with significant wealth disparity [3]. While it is a reliable destination for sadaqah, it maintains a medium donor fit for those seeking specific religious alignment as it is a secular service provider [6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high direct-service impact by rescuing food and providing meals to thousands of monthly clients [3][5]. While it maintains a high program expense ratio of 94.8%, the cost per beneficiary is approximately $528.78, reflecting the extreme poverty challenges in a high-cost region [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley provides essential food resources to one of the most expensive regions in the United States through a highly efficient operational model.
strengths: Exceptional financial efficiency with a fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity position with 9.5 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley works with school districts and community partners to maximize federally funded school meals and distribute groceries to those in need [1][3]. The organization reported a total revenue of $286,205,684 while maintaining a program expense ratio of 94.8% [2][4].

credibility.explanation: Donors can place high trust in this organization due to its <cite id="12">20/20 BBB standards compliance</cite>, <cite id="11">Candid Platinum status</cite>, and <cite id="18">audited financial statements</cite>.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="5">94.6% program ratio</cite> and a direct service model that <cite id="2">delivered 94 million meals</cite> last year.
impact.improvement: To improve impact scores, the charity could work to reduce the <cite id="16">$528.78 cost per beneficiary</cite> through further logistical optimizations.

rich_narrative.headline: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley provides essential food resources to one of the most expensive regions in the United States through a highly efficient operational model.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support high-efficiency, large-scale domestic poverty relief in one of the most economically disparate regions of the US.
donor_motivations: Value extreme operational efficiency ($0.03 fundraising cost)
Want to support local community members in Silicon Valley
Prefer organizations with the highest possible transparency ratings
Interested in food rescue and environmental sustainability
giving_considerations: Donations are sadaqah-eligible but not verified for zakat
The organization is a large institution, not a small grassroots collective
Focus is strictly domestic within two California counties
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking international impact or those specifically looking for zakat-verified pathways.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The charity tracks <cite id="23">4 distinct outcome metrics</cite> over multiple years and publishes <cite id="18">audited financial statements</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: SHSV aims to transition from crisis response to sustainable community health by providing nutritious food and connecting residents to long-term federal benefits.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that food distribution actually reduces hunger and improves health outcomes for the 94 million meals provided.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite high revenue, the cost of living and food prices in Silicon Valley continue to rise, exceeding current capacity.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Santa Clara and San Mateo Counties

peer_comparison.differentiator: SHSV maintains a <cite id="5">94.6% program ratio</cite>, which is significantly higher than the <cite id="13">peer median of 89.6%</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="9">fundraising efficiency of $0.03 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of contributions go directly to feeding the hungry.
SHSV holds <cite id="10">9.5 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant safety net to maintain services during economic downturns.
The charity holds a <cite id="11">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and meets <cite id="12">all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley's operational model is built on high-volume efficiency and strategic partnerships. By maintaining a <cite id="5">94.8% program expense ratio</cite>, the organization significantly exceeds the <cite id="13">peer program ratio median of 89.6%</cite> for similar humanitarian entities. Their governance is equally strong, featuring an <cite id="14">independent board of 17 members</cite> and a <cite id="15">conflict of interest policy</cite> that ensures ethical oversight. This institutional maturity allows them to manage a complex supply chain that <cite id="8">rescues 84 million pounds of food</cite> annually while keeping administrative costs to a minimum.
rich_narrative.summary: Second Harvest of Silicon Valley (SHSV) is a critical humanitarian institution that has served the San Francisco Bay Area since its <cite id="1">founding in 1974</cite>. Operating in one of the nation's most expensive regions, the organization utilizes a highly efficient model to <cite id="2">provide 94 million meals</cite> annually to residents facing food insecurity. The charity demonstrates exceptional financial stewardship, maintaining a <cite id="3">perfect 100/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, which reflects its commitment to transparency and operational excellence. In the 2023 fiscal year, SHSV managed a <cite id="4">total revenue of $286,205,684</cite>, with a remarkable <cite id="5">94.8% program expense ratio</cite> dedicated to direct service delivery. Beyond simple food distribution, the organization works strategically with school districts to <cite id="6">maximize federally funded school meals</cite> and conducts extensive <cite id="7">CalFresh outreach</cite> to ensure vulnerable populations access available government resources. Their scale is further evidenced by the <cite id="8">84 million pounds of food rescued</cite> from being wasted, redirecting it instead to community partners and grocery programs across Santa Clara and San Mateo counties.
